Container.Name: BombVault
Container.Overview: BombVault is a single-binary, Unraid-native app for one-click backup & restore of your Docker containers, powered by restic. Back up a container's appdata + definition with one click; restore it just as easily and the container reappears in the Docker tab exactly as before. Backup paths, encryption on/off, and the schedule are set IN the WebUI under Settings — not as container variables. WARNING: BombVault holds root-equivalent control of your host. Through the mounted docker.sock it can stop/start/recreate containers, and through the broad /mnt/user mount it reads appdata for backup and writes it back on restore. Run it ONLY on a trusted, non-exposed network. Optional password protection is available in Settings (off by default). Generate the required APP_KEY with: openssl rand -hex 32 (keep it safe — it derives the restic password, so losing it makes encrypted backups unrecoverable).
Container.DonateText: If this template helps you, consider buying me a coffee.

Config.Name: WebUI Port (HTTPS)
Config.Description: The BombVault WebUI (HTTPS, self-signed cert out of the box). This is the port you open.

Config.Name: WebUI Port (HTTP)
Config.Description: Plain-HTTP port. Only used when HTTP_ONLY is set to true (i.e. behind a TLS-terminating reverse proxy). Leave at the default otherwise.

Config.Name: HTTP_ONLY
Config.Description: Serve plain HTTP instead of HTTPS — only for use behind a TLS-terminating reverse proxy. false = HTTPS (recommended); true = plain HTTP on the HTTP port above.

Config.Name: APP_KEY
Config.Description: REQUIRED. 64 lowercase hex chars: openssl rand -hex 32. Master key for secrets + the derived restic password — losing it makes encrypted backups unrecoverable.

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: BombVault's own state (SQLite DB, TLS cert, restic cache).

Config.Name: Host Data
Config.Description: Your Unraid storage root /mnt — covers the user share AND cache/pool mounts, so any container's appdata (wherever it lives) is reachable. Backup SOURCES (container appdata) and DESTINATIONS both live here; pick the exact subpaths in the WebUI under Settings. Read for backup, written on restore.

Config.Name: Unraid Flash (/boot)
Config.Description: The Unraid USB flash, mounted whole. Powers two things: (1) Flash backup (Settings - Domains - Flash) of the entire /boot - Unraid OS, license, array config, shares, network and plugin config; and (2) capturing/restoring a container's Unraid template (under /boot/config/plugins/dockerMan/templates-user) so a restored container reappears as a NORMAL, editable Unraid app instead of a 'third-party' container. Leave at the default. Flash RESTORE never overwrites the live flash - it extracts to a folder you then copy onto a fresh USB.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Root-equivalent host control: stop/start/recreate containers around backup/restore.

Config.Name: HOST_SOURCE_ROOT
Config.Description: The HOST path mounted as 'Host Data' (default /mnt). BombVault translates the bind-mount sources Docker reports (e.g. /mnt/user/appdata/x) into paths under this mount. Change only if you mounted a different host root than /mnt.

Config.Name: VM Backup: Host
Config.Description: VM backup only. Unraid host address, reached over SSH. Leave default on bridge networking; on a custom (br0.x) network set your Unraid LAN IP (e.g. 192.168.10.10).

Config.Name: VM Backup: SSH Port
Config.Description: VM backup only. Unraid's SSH port. Change only if you changed it from 22.

Config.Name: VM Backup: SSH User
Config.Description: VM backup only. SSH user on the host. Leave as root.
